notes: Opening opportunity openings:plaintiff. Objective: plaintiff opening for proposition that by 2026-05-24 23:59 ET Iran initiated a major non-weather airspace closure within market rule. Checked get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence: only visible admitted evidence is ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, market-page.txt, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, size 4971, text/plain, system case_packet. Read full bytes with read_evidence_range. Admitted case packet proves rule elements and source hierarchy, not occurrence facts: Iran initiation; non-weather; by May 24 11:59 PM ET; major closure as broad closure/cancellation/suspension for commercial transiting/arriving/departing in Iran or major Iranian region, or complete suspension of commercial arrivals/departures affecting at least two listed airports; limited partial closures do not qualify; limited exceptions to broad closure do not defeat qualification; third-party restrictions/weather-only do not qualify; primary sources are official Iranian aviation info and consensus credible reporting. Native investigation: searched web for Iran closed airspace May 24 2026 / NOTAM / Reuters / Polymarket. Polymarket current page repeats rule and shows May 24 final outcome No; treat as adverse lead, not proof of factual condition. Searched exact late-May NOTAM phrase. Leads found: Indian Express and Siasat report Iranian CAA/CAO NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22-May 25 closing western Tehran FIR/airports, leaving eight airports including IKA, THR, IFN, SYZ, MHD operational daylight-only with per-flight CAA approval. These leads support plaintiff theory if admitted/corroborated later, but also show adverse issue: partial versus qualifying major regional closure. Opened Iran International URL mentioned in an adverse Manifold snippet; fetched page was unrelated to airspace and did not substantiate fake-news claim. Durable notes saved at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Filing decision: opening should not claim unadmitted late-May facts as established. It should frame the legal dispute, explain what the case packet proves/does not prove, identify source targets for later evidence submission, and acknowledge strongest adverse theory (partial restrictions/platform No outcome) while preserving plaintiff theory that a broad western FIR shutdown with only limited approval/daylight exceptions can satisfy the rule.

Turn: openings:defendant. Objective: defendant opening statement. Inspected current record with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only visible evidence is ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, case-packet text, 4971 bytes. Plaintiff opening on record. Packet proves rule/market metadata only, not occurrence facts. Rule elements: Iran-initiated, non-weather, by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET; major means broad commercial flight closure/cancellation/suspension for transit/arrival/departure across Iran or a major Iranian airspace region, or complete commercial arrivals/departures suspension affecting at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Limited cancellations/delays/partial closures do not qualify; limited exceptions to an otherwise broad closure do not disqualify. Source map: decisive proof is official Iranian CAA NOTAM/equivalent exact text, effective/expiry times, affected FIR/airports/routes, treatment of overflight, arrivals, departures, and scope of exceptions. Defendant proof would include continued ordinary operations, only localized/partial restrictions, third-party warnings/avoidance, or source-chain defects. Targeted searches performed: Iran airspace closure May 24 2026 NOTAM western airspace Tehran FIR; site:notams.aim.faa.gov Iran Tehran FIR May 24 2026 NOTAM airspace closure; Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 flights approval civil aviation authority; "OIIX A1010/26"; "A1010/26" "OIIX"; "May 22" "May 25" "OIIX" "A1010/26". Leads found in search results: alleged OIIX A1010/26, May 22-May 25, western Tehran FIR, daylight/per-flight approval exceptions, eight operational airports including several listed airports; secondary/social sources include Indian Express/Siasat/Kenya Times/SafeAirspace/EASA/social posts. No official primary NOTAM captured/admitted yet; opening should not cite those leads as proof. Adverse check: if official NOTAM shows broad western FIR closure with only limited permissions, plaintiff may have a strong path under broad-region language. Defendant opening should emphasize burden and missing source chain, exact scope, and continued-operation/partial-exception issues without overclaiming no closure occurred. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Arguments opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Rescanned record with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence: before plaintiff submissions the record had only ev_e61df84283cc_market-page plus openings; no new evidence or changed metadata. Defendant opening focuses on exact source/scope and argues no official NOTAM text yet. Investigation followed opening search map. Opened Indian Express page and Siasat page. Submitted two source extracts through submit_evidence before relying on them. Accepted IDs: ev_6877e32e36e2_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-6877e32e36e2 (Indian Express report, May 23 2026; reports CAA NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22 valid until May 25, western Tehran FIR airspace/airports closure, suspension of civil flight permits in western OIIX, only eight airports operational under daylight/per-flight approval; flight tracking rerouting); ev_25136676d648_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-25136676d648 (Siasat corroborating report, May 23 2026; identifies NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, airports across western Tehran FIR closed, limited daylight facilities, fresh CAA approval required, non-weather military/diplomatic context). Search/source limitations: no official NOTAM artifact obtained; Indian Express cites Gulf News for NOTAM details and Siasat is secondary. Do not overclaim listed-airport complete suspension because Indian Express says IKA, THR, IFN, SYZ, MHD were among eight airports still operational with restrictions. Stronger argument is broad major Iranian airspace region: western sector of Tehran FIR, all airports in western part shutdown, civil flight permits suspended, with approval/daylight permissions as limited exceptions. Durable journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Planned filing offers PX-1 case packet, PX-2 Indian Express, PX-3 Siasat; includes technical report source ledger noting official NOTAM unavailable and next searches.

arguments:defendant work notes. Inspected current record and evidence list. Plaintiff added ev_6877e32e36e2 (Indian Express report capture) and ev_25136676d648 (Siasat report capture). Stat/read performed for both. Exact contents: Indian Express says only eight Iranian airports remained operational under daylight restrictions; reports Iran temporarily closed airspace over western half May 22-May 25, suspending all civil flight permits in western OIIX; says Iranian CAA issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 on May 22 shutting all airports in western Tehran FIR, citing Gulf News; says operational airports included Tehran Mehrabad, IKA, Isfahan, Shiraz, Yazd, Tabriz, Mashhad, Bandar Abbas and required daylight/fresh per-flight CAA approval. Siasat says Iran imposed new restrictions May 23; identifies NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 announcing closure of airports across western Tehran FIR; limited facilities daylight; fresh CAA approval before passenger flights in affected areas; non-weather military/diplomatic context. Investigation: web queries for Iran says no new NOTAM/A1010/26/REF NOTAM A0796/26. Found and submitted Notamify raw text for A1010/26 and replacement A1015/26 as ev_5e5109b5ca63. It shows A1010 category AD Airport Operations, period 2026-05-22 20:46 to 2026-05-25 08:30 UTC; raw text: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW operational sunrise-sunset; previous permissions suspended; new permission requested from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Replacement A1015/26 effective 2026-05-23 18:21 added OIFM/OITL/OISR/OIAM exceptions. Also submitted Iran International report as ev_6057a17cc5bf: Iran CAO reportedly denied a new official notice imposing countrywide airspace restrictions, said flight conditions normal/flights according to schedule, while acknowledging earlier western Tehran FIR airport activity suspension with exemptions. Argument theory: plaintiff's evidence proves at most a partial/conditional airport-operations NOTAM, not a broad closure of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing from Iranian airspace or a major region. Listed-airport complete-suspension path fails because plaintiff and defense evidence show listed airports such as IKA/Mashhad/Shiraz/Isfahan remained operational in some form. Provenance/custody issue remains: no official CAA/ICAO original NOTAM admitted; Notamify is an aggregator, but its raw text is more precise than secondary article paraphrases and narrows scope. Adverse risk: council may treat airport closure in western Tehran FIR plus permission requirement as broad regional closure under limited-exceptions clause. Response: rule requires broad commercial transit/arrival/departure suspension and excludes partial closures; many named exceptions plus no proof of overflight/route closure keep plaintiff below preponderance.

Rebuttal opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff. Scanned new record/evidence. Defendant submitted DX1 ev_5e5109b5ca63_submitted-evidence-03-defendant-5e5109b5ca63 (Notamify raw A1010/26 and A1015/26) and DX2 ev_6057a17cc5bf_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-6057a17cc5bf (Iran International denial/complication). Used stat_evidence and read_evidence_range for both. DX1 exact captured text materially helps plaintiff on broad-major-region path: A1010 says ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR ARE CLSD EXCEPT listed airports operational sunrise-to-sunset; ALL PREVIOUS PERMISSIONS ARE SUSPENDED FOR ALL OPERATORS; new permission requested from CAA for civil IFR passenger flights. A1015 replaces A1010 and repeats all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except a longer list; all previous permissions suspended; new permission from Iran CAA for civil IFR passenger flights. DX2 is adverse but limited: it denies a new official aviation notice imposing countrywide restrictions/social-media notice and says conditions remain normal, but also says the denial came after Iran CAO said Friday that activity at airports in western Tehran FIR had been suspended until Monday, with only limited airports allowed to operate sunrise-to-sunset. Rebuttal plan: no new evidence needed; use defendant's DX1/DX2 as plaintiff-friendly or limited; concede listed-airport complete-suspension path is weaker; argue rule also covers broad closure of major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions do not defeat; defendant imports an overflight/ATS-route requirement not in rule. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

surrebuttals:defendant notes. Inspected record with get_case/list_evidence. No new source evidence added in plaintiff rebuttal; plaintiff instead offered defendant evidence as rebuttal exhibits PX-R1/PX-R2 and filed a targeted evidence read report. Rebuttal strongest points: DX-1 raw NOTAM text says all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except listed sunrise-to-sunset airports and all previous permissions suspended/new CAA permission required; plaintiff argues this is broad major-region closure with limited exceptions under rule. Plaintiff says rule does not require every route/altitude/overflight track to be separately closed. Plaintiff says DX-2 denies only a new nationwide/social-media notice but confirms earlier western Tehran FIR airport activity suspension. Surrebuttal response plan: concede DX-1 proves a real Iranian airport restriction by deadline, not weather. Emphasize plaintiff still must prove a major closure of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from airspace/major region or complete suspension at two listed airports. DX-1 is airport operations (QFALC/AD) and lacks FIR/overflight/route closure. Numerous and expanded exceptions plus affirmative permission for civil IFR passenger flights make this partial/conditional operations, not limited exceptions to an otherwise proven broad closure. DX-2 matters because it denies countrywide/airspace restrictions and confirms ambiguity/partial airport activity suspension. No new outside evidence needed for narrow surrebuttal.

Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Final action only deliver_closing_statement; no evidence submission or technical reports. Final record scan performed with get_case and list_evidence. Record now includes openings, merits arguments, plaintiff rebuttal, defendant surrebuttal, five evidence items, and three technical reports. No new evidence can be added. Defendant surrebuttal repeats strongest adverse point: DX1 is airport operations only, no ATS/overflight/route closure; exceptions/operational airports too numerous; DX2 warns against overreading. Plaintiff closing plan: concede listed-airport complete-suspension pathway is not strongest. Focus on broad-major-region pathway. Decisive evidence remains DX1 raw NOTAM wording: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except named airports; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; fresh Iran CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights; A1010/A1015 active before deadline. PX2/PX3 and DX2 corroborate western Tehran FIR airport activity suspension and non-weather/security context. Best response to adverse point: rule does not require total nationwide closure, complete closure of two listed airports if broad-region path is met, or explicit ATS route closure; commercial arrivals/departures in a major Iranian airspace region are broadly suspended where all airports are closed except limited daylight/permission exceptions. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

closings:defendant notes. Inspected current prompt/record and evidence list. Plaintiff closing added no new evidence; closing argues DX-1 raw NOTAM is decisive, broad-region pathway is sufficient, and airport closures plus suspension of prior permissions/fresh approval are limited exceptions to broad closure. No new search/admission allowed in closing. Defense synthesis: plaintiff bears burden to show qualifying interpretation is more likely than partial nonqualifying restriction. Best plaintiff fact is raw text: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR closed except named airports; prior permissions suspended; fresh CAA permission required. Defense response: PX-1 requires broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major region. DX-1 is airport-operations/QFALC text, not route/overflight/FIR closure evidence, and it affirmatively permits civil IFR passenger flights at numerous airports with approval. Listed-airport complete suspension path fails because listed airports remained operational in some form. Provenance remains imperfect: no official Iranian CAA/ICAO artifact, no airport movement data, no route/FIR closure exhibit. Closing should ask council to find proposition not proven; record shows a partial, conditional western airport restriction.
